questions.question: A `case` command returns failure by default when no pattern matches.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. return status of a case command is zero if no pattern matches.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: A variable created with `local` remains visibly scoped across the whole shell after the function returns.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. local builtin makes a variable's visible scope restricted to that function and its children.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If `select` reads an empty line, it displays the menu words and prompt again.
questions.explanation: Correct. If an empty line is read by select, it displays the words and prompt again.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If a `case` clause matches, the command's return status is the exit status of the last command-list executed.
questions.explanation: Correct. If a case command matches a clause, its return status is the exit status of the last command-list executed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If the requested command cannot be found, the `command` builtin returns status 0.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. command builtin gives status 127 if the requested command cannot be found or an error occurred.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: A shell script can be made executable by using `chmod` to turn on the execute bit.
questions.explanation: Correct. A shell script may be made executable by using chmod to turn on the execute bit.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The return status of an `if` command is always the status of its test command list.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. return status of an if command is the exit status of the last command executed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If `select` reads EOF, it completes and returns 1.
questions.explanation: Correct. If EOF is read, a select command completes and returns 1.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If no condition tested true, an `if` command returns zero.
questions.explanation: Correct. An if command gives zero if no condition tested true.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The raw line read by `select` is stored in the `PS3` variable.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. line read by a select command is saved in the REPLY variable.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In an `if` command, a zero status from the test command list causes the consequent command list to run.
questions.explanation: Correct. In an if command, if the test-commands list returns status zero, the consequent-commands list is executed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Inside a function, `declare` makes each declared name local unless `-g` is used.
questions.explanation: Correct. When used in a function, declare makes each name local unless the -g option is supplied.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `select` construct exists to make menu generation easier.
questions.explanation: Correct. select construct allows the easy generation of menus.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Shell functions always run in a separate process from the current shell.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. Shell functions are executed in the current shell context, and no new process is created to interpret them.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If an `else` clause is present, it can run when the prior `if` or `elif` conditions do not succeed.
questions.explanation: Correct. If the final command in the final if or elif clause has a non-zero exit status and an else clause is present, the alternate-consequents are executed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The special parameter `$?` expands to the output text of the most recently executed command.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. special parameter $? expands to the exit status of the most recently executed command.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: When `local` is called with no operands, it clears all existing local variables.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. With no operands, the local builtin writes a list of local variables to standard output.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If `select` reads a non-empty invalid value, it keeps the selection variable unchanged.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If select reads any other value that is not a valid menu number, it sets the selected name to null.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Shell functions group commands for later execution under a single name.
questions.explanation: Correct. Shell functions are a way to group commands for later execution using a single name for the group.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In an `if` command, `elif` branches are skipped unless the initial test command returned zero.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If the test-commands list of an if command returns non-zero, each elif list is executed in turn, and the matching consequent runs when one returns zero.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: When Bash runs a shell script, special parameter `$0` is set to the script file name rather than the shell name.
questions.explanation: Correct. When Bash runs a shell script, it sets the special parameter 0 to the name of the file rather than the name of the shell.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `local` builtin can be used at top level outside of any function as long as the variable name is valid.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. local builtin can only be used within a function.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Using `declare +r` removes the readonly attribute from a readonly variable.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. Using +r with declare does not remove the readonly attribute.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If a shell script is run with no additional arguments, its positional parameters are automatically set to empty strings.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If no additional arguments are supplied to a shell script, the positional parameters are unset.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: After a name is made readonly, later assignment or `unset` on that name is not allowed.
questions.explanation: Correct. Readonly names cannot be assigned values by subsequent assignment statements or unset.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `command` builtin can execute shell builtins or commands found through `PATH` lookup.
questions.explanation: Correct. command builtin executes only shell builtin commands or commands found by searching PATH.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: When Bash finds an executable shell script through `PATH`, it always runs it in the current shell without creating a new Bash instance.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. When Bash finds an executable shell script while searching PATH for a command, it creates a new instance of itself to execute it.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If a `select` command omits the word list, it behaves as if no menu items exist at all.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If the word list is omitted in a select command, Bash uses the positional parameters as if in "$@" had been specified.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Inside a shell function named `ls`, using `command ls` can execute the external `ls` instead of recursing into the function.
questions.explanation: Correct. If there is a shell function named ls, running command ls within that function executes the external command ls instead of calling the function recursively.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The special parameter `$#` expands to the number of positional parameters.
questions.explanation: Correct. special parameter $# expands to the number of positional parameters in decimal.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
